pi_items.source: Task descriptions and evaluator scripts explicitly define and check this path.
Task descriptions and evaluator scripts explicitly define and check this path.
Task scripts explicitly use this path to set up the task and compute ground truth. Validated by extracting HEC-RAS v6.6 linux build which contains this exact project file.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the official HEC-RAS Muncie example (White River, Indiana). The geometry data in the HDF file contains a single 2D flow area named '2D Interior Area' with exactly 5,765 computational cells. The dataset 'Cells Center Coordinate' provides X,Y coordinates, bounding roughly from X: 404,980 to 412,215 and Y: 1,801,020 to 1,805,021. The dataset 'Cells Minimum Elevation' contains 5,765 elevation values, but crucially, 374 of these are NaN (including the last cell, index 5764). The benchmark's ground truth generator uses standard numpy functions (like np.min) without handling NaNs, causing the generated ground truth for min, max, mean, and std elevation to all evaluate as NaN. Because the verifier script checks these numerical values using absolute differences, the comparison against NaN will always evaluate to False. As a result, agents will invariably fail the evaluation for the summary elevation statistics and the spot-check for the last CSV sample, even if they perfectly extract the valid numbers. The valid (non-NaN) minimum elevation is approximately 925.0.
